meta.title: Mercedes-Benz OM601-OM608 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz OM601-OM608 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The OM601–608 family are the legendary over-engineered Mercedes diesel fours, fives and sixes — renowned for million‑mile durability, though injection pump and glow plug issues are well known. Backed by real UK enquiries [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: Confidence: 90% confidence — based on decades of owner feedback, specialist workshop consensus, and extensive forum data; proprietary enquiry figures are estimated.

rows.metric: Most repairs are straightforward; injection pumps and glow plugs are well understood, but some parts (injection pumps) are expensive

rows.value: Widely available aftermarket and genuine NOS, but some specific items are getting scarce

rows.metric: Depends on host vehicle value — classic/collector values may justify, but everyday examples may approach scrap threshold

rows.value: Sum of the five rows above

rows.metric: One-line verdict: The OM60x diesels are among the most durable engines ever built — but ageing injection pumps and glow plug systems need care — buy well‑maintained examples with documented service history.
rows.value: Best For: Classic Mercedes enthusiasts, high‑mileage drivers, and anyone seeking legendary diesel longevity.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Cross-link: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an OM60x engine typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED]. Injection pump calibration is a specialist job.
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand cost comparison applies.

cta.label: → Compare OM601–608 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: How much does an OM601–608 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned OM60x engine typically costs £2,000–£3,500 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by cylinder count (I4, I5, I6) and turbo vs naturally aspirated. Fitting adds £550–£1,100 depending on labour rates.

items.question: Is the OM601–608 a reliable engine?
items.answer: The OM60x series are legendary for durability, earning 4.5★ out of 5 [ESTIMATED] — million‑mile examples exist. Regular oil changes and injector pump maintenance are the key to longevity.

items.question: What is the most common OM601–608 failure?
items.answer: Mechanical injection pump wear and glow plug failure are the most common issues — difficult starting, rough idle, and reduced power typically appear after 150,000–200,000 miles. Rebuild kits are available.

items.question: Which Mercedes models use the OM601–608?
items.answer: The OM60x family spans 190D (W201), E‑Class (W124/W210), S‑Class (W126/W140), G‑Class, Sprinter, and more — truly the backbone of Mercedes diesel production from 1980 to 2001.

items.question: How long does the OM601–608 last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, 300,000–500,000 miles is common, and many exceed 1,000,000 miles. The engines are overbuilt; the rest of the car usually fails first. Regular injector pump servicing is essential.
